digest.component_or_area: release pipeline build-once artifact identity + Hatchling provenance hook
propositions: The published wheel built via the default sdist-to-wheel path bakes COMMIT = None because the sdist has no .git and the hook overwrites unconditionally.
The release artifact is never installed or functionally tested before publication, so the wheel-from-sdist defect goes uncaught by existing gates.
The provenance hook should resolve the commit by a four-step precedence: REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT env var, preserve existing non-null COMMIT, git rev-parse --short HEAD, then None.
The build-once flow builds wheel+sdist once, tests the exact artifacts in isolated jobs, and publishes the same bytes verified via sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS with no rebuild.
The commit format must be a short SHA to stay consistent with signing.py's short-SHA convention across install types.
The hook must fail the build only when REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT is set but empty/unresolvable, while an unset env var falls back gracefully to git for dev installs.
